science.title: Scientists just created exotic new forms of matter that shouldn’t exist
science.summary: A new quantum physics study reveals that simply changing a magnetic field over time can unlock entirely new forms of matter that don’t exist under normal conditions. science.title: Scientists Discover a New Way To Control Metals at the Atomic Scale
science.summary: The discovery could help make electronics faster and more energy efficient. Researchers at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities have found a new way to change how a metal behaves electronically by controlling atomic-level interactions at the boundary where two materials meet. science.title: Scientists Turn Cancer’s Own Bacteria Against It in Breakthrough Therapy
science.summary: A newly developed therapy inspired by bacteria residing within tumors offers a different way to combat cancer by targeting how tumor cells produce energy. science.title: Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe
science.summary: Scientists may have uncovered a surprising secret behind why life exists at all. science.title: Scientists found the “holy grail” gene that could one day help humans regrow limbs
science.summary: Scientists studying axolotls, zebrafish, and mice have uncovered a shared set of genes that may one day help humans regrow lost limbs. tech.title: Engineers at NASA's Jet Propulsion Lab make a breakthrough in rotor technology
tech.summary: Testing shows rotor blades won't disintegrate when they spin at supersonic speed.

tech.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
tech.summary: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to make painful cuts — including our subsidiaries."

medicine.title: A common constipation drug shows surprising power to protect kidneys
medicine.summary: A common constipation drug may have unexpectedly unlocked a new way to slow chronic kidney disease — a condition that affects millions and often leads to dialysis. medicine.title: Colon cancer breakthrough keeps patients cancer-free for nearly 3 years
medicine.summary: A short burst of immunotherapy before surgery is delivering surprisingly powerful results for a specific type of colorectal cancer. medicine.title: New treatment cuts bad cholesterol by nearly 50% without statins
medicine.summary: A new breakthrough could change how high cholesterol is treated, offering a powerful alternative to traditional drugs. Researchers have developed tiny DNA-based molecules that shut down PCSK9—a key protein that keeps “bad” LDL cholesterol circulating in the blood. medicine.title: Breakthrough biomaterial heals tissue from the inside out
medicine.summary: Scientists have developed a breakthrough injectable biomaterial that travels through the bloodstream to repair damaged tissue from within, reducing inflammation and jumpstarting healing. medicine.title: New obesity discovery rewrites decades of fat science
medicine.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising secret hidden inside fat cells that could reshape how we think about obesity and metabolic disease. mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. astronomy.title: How Massive Star Clusters Shape Galaxy Evolution

astronomy.summary: A spiral galaxy seen close up and tilted at an angle, so that its disc fills the view from corner to corner. Its disc is yellow near to the centre and pale blue farther out, showing cooler and hotter stars, respectively. astronomy.title: How an exoplanet odd couple survived by traveling in from the cold together
astronomy.summary: By probing the atmosphere of a mini-Neptune exoplanet, the James Webb Space Telescope has found that it formed much farther from its star than it is today, possibly explaining the origin of many other mini-Neptunes in the process.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 8 – 17
astronomy.summary: Jupiter, shining high the western dusk, inches down day toward brighter Venus. Venus, meanwhile, creeps toward the horntip stars of Taurus and stands between them on May 13th. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 8 – 17 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Nearby Super-Earth Has No Atmosphere and a Dark, Moon-like Surface
astronomy.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed the surface of a bare rocky exoplanet, revealing old, dark rock akin to that on our Moon. The post Nearby Super-Earth Has No Atmosphere and a Dark, Moon-like Surface appeared first on Sky & Telescope.
